companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVDA bounced to ~$202 (+3.2% from June 25's $195.74), partly lifted by CoreWeave and IREN joining the Russell 3000 at today's market close — NVIDIA holds approximately an 11% stake in CoreWeave and a $2.1B investment option in IREN. Vera Rubin mass production is confirmed, with OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX among first customers; H2 2026 ramp begins in the quarter ending October 2026. Annual Meeting proxy results (Form 8-K) are due by June 30 — 4 days away.

items.headline: NVDA at ~$202 (+3.2%) — CoreWeave and IREN join Russell 3000 at today's close; NVIDIA holds stakes in both
items.detail: NVIDIA shares bounced to approximately $202 on June 26 (opened $202.17, prior close ~$199) as CoreWeave (CRWV) and IREN Limited join the Russell 3000 index effective at today's market close. NVIDIA holds approximately an 11% stake (~$2B) in CoreWeave and a $2.1 billion investment option in IREN. The dual Russell 3000 inclusions trigger mandatory passive-fund buying beginning June 29 and increase index visibility for NVIDIA's broader infrastructure investment network. Updated indices begin trading June 27–29.

items.headline: Vera Rubin mass production confirmed — OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX among first customers; H2 2026 ramp begins
items.detail: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang confirmed Vera Rubin entered full production. OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX (via Colossus) are among the first confirmed Vera Rubin customers, with shipping beginning in the quarter ending October 2026. Analysts project Vera Rubin will outperform the Blackwell revenue ramp. The first gigawatt of Vera Rubin cloud capacity for OpenAI is targeted for H2 2026 under the 10GW NVIDIA-OpenAI partnership.

items.headline: NVIDIA Annual Meeting proxy results due by June 30 — 4 days away
items.detail: NVIDIA held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders on June 24. Proxy voting results will be filed via Form 8-K within four business days — by June 30. Seven proposals were on the ballot: director elections, PwC ratification, executive compensation, and four shareholder proposals covering simple majority voting, faith-based workforce reporting, civil liberties/DEI reporting, and Scope 3 emissions disclosure. FY2026 results highlighted in proxy: $215.9B revenue (+65% YoY), 71.1% gross margin, diluted EPS $4.90.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro now has 4 days to Sundar Pichai's self-imposed June 30 window, but CryptoBriefing reports insider indications of a July slip and prediction markets are now below 50%. Gemini 2.5 Pro with Deep Think (launched June 22) remains the top-ranked model on GPQA Diamond at 82.4%, above Fable 5 and GPT-5.5. Google DeepMind faces its most significant talent losses of 2026: Nobel laureate John Jumper departed for Anthropic and Noam Shazeer (Gemini co-lead) is reportedly departing for OpenAI. GOOGL ~$343.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro: 4 days to June 30 deadline — likely slipping to July; prediction markets now below 50%
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains in limited Vertex AI enterprise preview as of June 26. CryptoBriefing reported insider indications that the launch may slip to July 2026 — the first publicly missed model window from Google DeepMind this year. Prediction markets have moved below 50% odds for a June 30 launch. Google declined to comment on a revised timeline. Expected specs when released: 2M token context window, Deep Think reasoning mode, Deep Research integration, pricing approximately $15 per million input tokens.

items.headline: Gemini 2.5 Pro with Deep Think holds #1 on GPQA Diamond at 82.4% — above Fable 5 and GPT-5.5
items.detail: Gemini 2.5 Pro with Deep Think reasoning mode (launched June 22) posts 82.4% on GPQA Diamond science-reasoning benchmarks — currently the highest published score, above Anthropic Fable 5 (79.1%, suspended) and OpenAI GPT-5.5 (76.3%). Available for Google AI Ultra subscribers ($250/month). Deep Think mode runs at approximately 4x standard token cost. The model features a 2 million token context window and is available via the Gemini API.

items.headline: Google DeepMind facing most significant talent losses of 2026 — Jumper to Anthropic, Shazeer reportedly to OpenAI
items.detail: Nobel laureate John Jumper — AlphaFold2 co-creator and 2024 Nobel Prize winner in Chemistry — departed Google DeepMind after 9 years to join Anthropic (announced June 19). His first public Anthropic appearance is confirmed for June 30. Noam Shazeer — VP Engineering and Gemini co-lead — is reportedly departing for OpenAI, though this has not been officially confirmed. Both departures coincide with the Gemini 3.5 Pro delay and represent the sharpest talent headwinds DeepMind has faced this year.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Copilot Cowork billing grace period for Frontier preview tenants ends July 1 — now 5 days away. Microsoft and Anthropic continue active negotiations to run Claude on Maia 200 custom AI chips via Azure; if finalized, Anthropic would be the first external Maia 200 customer. Microsoft IQ is generally available across GitHub Copilot, Foundry, and Copilot Studio. Microsoft continues exploring fine-tuned open-source alternatives (DeepSeek or comparable) for some Copilot Cowork workloads to reduce frontier API costs at agentic token scale.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork grace period ends July 1 — 5 days away; configure usage-based billing or lose access
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork reached general availability June 16 under a Copilot Credits usage-based billing model. Frontier preview tenants have a grace period until July 1 before billing activates; organizations without usage-based billing configured lose Copilot Cowork access. Microsoft 365 Business Standard and Business Premium with Copilot formalize as permanent commercial SKUs on the same date. Copilot Cowork runs on Anthropic Opus 4.8 and Sonnet 4.6 at GA, with GPT-5.5 available for Frontier customers.

items.headline: Anthropic-Microsoft Maia 200 chip negotiations ongoing — no deal closed; Maia 200 running in Arizona and Iowa data centers
items.detail: Microsoft and Anthropic remain in active negotiations to deploy Claude for inference on Maia 200 custom AI accelerators via Azure. If finalized, Anthropic would be the first external customer to run on Maia 200, which Microsoft says can achieve more than 30% better performance-per-dollar than NVIDIA hardware for inference. Maia 200 chips are now running in Microsoft's Arizona and Iowa data centers. A deal would extend Anthropic's multi-silicon strategy — currently spanning SpaceX/Colossus, AWS Trainium, CoreWeave, and Google/Broadcom TPU — to include Microsoft in-house silicon.

items.headline: Microsoft IQ generally available; exploring open-source model alternatives for Copilot Cowork workloads
items.detail: Microsoft IQ — Work IQ APIs (organizational context inside the M365 trust boundary) and Web IQ (real-time web grounding) — is generally available across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio. MAI-Thinking-1 (35B parameter reasoning model) and MAI-Image-2.5 are the newest in-house models from Build 2026. Axios reported Microsoft is evaluating hosting a fine-tuned DeepSeek or comparable open-source model for some Copilot Cowork workloads to reduce Anthropic and OpenAI API costs at agentic token scale — not a model change for enterprise Foundry or flagship Copilot products.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 did not launch during the June 22–28 prediction market window; odds have collapsed from 83% to ~18% and July is now the consensus launch window (~94% probability by end of July). The 42-state AG coalition probe (led by NY AG Letitia James) served a subpoena around June 13, targeting advertising practices, user data, sycophancy, and minor protections. OpenAI's IPO is reportedly leaning toward 2027 — CFO Sarah Friar is citing operational readiness and SpaceX IPO chilling effects. ChatGPT global market share fell below 50% for the first time (46.4%).

items.headline: GPT-5.6 not launched — June 22–28 odds collapsed to ~18%; July now ~94% probability by end of month
items.detail: OpenAI published no announcement for GPT-5.6 as of June 26. Prediction market odds for the June 22–28 window have collapsed to approximately 18%. The consensus has shifted to a July 2026 launch window, with about 94% probability by end of July. GPT-5.6 is expected to address the 'Goblin Incident' reward model failure with a redesigned reward audit pipeline, feature up to 1.5–2 million token context (larger than GPT-5.5), and achieve approximately 10–15% token efficiency gains. Chief Scientist Jakub Pachocki described it as a 'meaningful improvement.'

items.headline: 42-state AG coalition probe formally served subpoena; OpenAI cooperating — targets ads, data practices, sycophancy, minors
items.detail: A coalition of 42 state attorneys general led by NY AG Letitia James formally opened a probe of OpenAI around June 12–13, days after the confidential S-1 filing, and served a subpoena. The investigation covers advertising practices, user data handling, treatment of minors and seniors, model sycophancy, and safety policies. OpenAI says it is cooperating and engaging 'constructively.' The probe substantially complicates the roadshow narrative for any near-term IPO and is cited as one of CFO Sarah Friar's reasons for preferring a 2027 listing.

items.headline: OpenAI Patch the Planet (launched June 22) found 51 open-source issues; ChatGPT market share below 50% for first time
items.detail: OpenAI's Patch the Planet initiative — launched June 22 in partnership with Trail of Bits and HackerOne — uses GPT-5.5-Cyber to automatically identify, write, test, and merge security fixes for widely used open-source projects. First results: 19 projects targeted including Linux, Chrome V8, cURL, Python, Go, freenginx, and Sigstore; 51 issues found, 19 already patched. Separately, Sensor Tower data showed ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% — first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain suspended on Day 14. The Commerce Department deadline (June 26) passed with no public written response from Secretary Lutnick. Prediction markets moved up to 68–71% odds of US restoration before July 1. Trump stated negotiations are 'going fine' and The Next Web reported Anthropic floated a verified US-only access gating proposal to Lutnick. Next structural milestones: July 1 (Polymarket deadline), July 8 (Persona biometric ID pathway in Anthropic privacy policy update), August 1 (EO-linked AI risk framework deadline). AI for Science virtual event is June 30 at 10 AM PST — 4 days away. Series H confirmed closed at $65B, $965B post-money (May 28). October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target.

items.headline: Fable 5 ban Day 14 — Commerce Dept June 26 deadline passed with no public Lutnick response; Anthropic floated US-only access proposal; prediction markets 68–71% odds of restoration before July 1
items.detail: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain globally suspended on Day 14 (June 26). The June 26 Commerce Department deadline — set by four bipartisan House members who demanded written justification under 14 C.F.R. § 744.22(b) — passed with no public response from Secretary Lutnick. The Next Web reported Anthropic floated a 'crisis talks' proposal to end the ban via verified US-citizen-only access gating; no official confirmation. President Trump said at the G7 that negotiations are 'going fine.' Prediction markets rose to 68–71% odds of US restoration before July 1. Next key milestones: July 1 (Polymarket settlement), July 8 (Anthropic privacy policy update enabling Persona biometric ID verification as US-only restoration pathway), August 1 (60-day executive order deadline for NSA/Treasury/CISA AI risk framework).
items.sourceTitle: The Next Web / Globe and Mail

items.headline: AI for Science virtual event June 30 — 4 days away; John Jumper (Nobel laureate) first public Anthropic appearance; life sciences showcases
items.detail: Anthropic's 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event is confirmed for June 30 at 10 AM PST — 4 days away. Nobel Prize laureate and AlphaFold2 co-creator John Jumper (departed Google DeepMind after 9 years, joined Anthropic June 19) makes his first public Anthropic appearance at the event alongside Andrej Karpathy (joined May 19, leading Claude-assisted pre-training). The event features life sciences executives, research institutions, and product and research demonstrations. Livestream open to the public; applications for in-person attendance closed.

items.headline: Series H confirmed closed at $65B, $965B post-money (May 28); S-1 filed June 1; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target; Maia 200 negotiations continuing
items.detail: Anthropic's Series H closed May 28 at $65 billion raised, $965 billion post-money valuation — the highest-valued private AI company, surpassing OpenAI. Co-led by Sequoia Capital, Dragoneer, Altimeter, and Greenoaks, with Blackstone, Brookfield, D.E. Shaw, Baillie Gifford, and Fidelity also participating. Anthropic's confidential Form S-1 was submitted to the SEC June 1, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO at a projected ~$1.1 trillion first-day market cap. Annual revenue run rate exceeds $30 billion. Maia 200 chip negotiations with Microsoft remain ongoing — no deal closed.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta Glasses (launched June 23, $299–$399) are receiving strong early reviews; smart glasses shipments surged 167% YoY in Q1 2026 with Meta at 69.2% market share. Meta Llama 4 continues to emerge as the leading self-hostable frontier-class alternative as the Fable 5 ban enters Day 14 — open-weights posture is now a procurement variable following the June 12 suspension. 2026 capex guidance stands at $115–145B. AMD MI450 samples are shipping under the $60B/6GW partnership; CoreWeave's $21B agreement is proceeding.

items.headline: Meta Glasses launch receiving strong reviews — $299 at launch; 167% YoY smart glasses growth; 69.2% market share
items.detail: Meta launched in-house designed Meta Glasses with EssilorLuxottica on June 23 at $299 (Fury and Adventurer lines) and $399 (Starfire; also a Kylie Jenner edition). Launch features include Muse Spark AI, live translation expanded to 20 languages (Mandarin, Korean, Japanese, Arabic, Hindi), and dynamic photos burst mode. Smart glasses shipments surged 167% year-over-year in Q1 2026, with Meta at 69.2% market share. Competing designs from Google/Warby Parker, Samsung/Gentle Monster, and Apple are incoming.

items.headline: Meta Llama 4 is leading enterprise self-hostable frontier alternative during Fable 5 ban — Day 14
items.detail: As Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain suspended into Day 14, Meta Llama 4 has emerged as the leading self-hostable frontier-class model that enterprises are evaluating as a hedge against government model access revocation risk. Open-weights posture is now a formal procurement variable for large organizations following the June 12 suspension. CEO Arthur Mensch of Mistral AI has made parallel sovereignty arguments. Both reflect the industry's new awareness that centralized model access can be revoked by government directive.

items.headline: Meta AI Mode on Facebook; 2026 capex $115–145B; AMD MI450 samples shipping; CoreWeave $21B deal proceeding
items.detail: Meta launched an AI Mode on Facebook that pulls from public information across its platforms for context-aware recommendations inside Facebook search and feed, using a three-tier model routing system. Meta's 2026 capital expenditure guidance remains $115–145 billion, supporting the AMD 6GW partnership, CoreWeave $21B agreement through December 2032, and broader AI infrastructure buildout. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to Meta under the $60B/6GW multi-generation partnership.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: Bedrock AgentCore is SOC 1/2/3 compliant with failure, intent, and trajectory insights now live since June 17. The Cerebras-AWS partnership (announced June 23) adds Cerebras CS-3 as a third silicon tier for ultra-fast inference decode alongside Trainium and NVIDIA GPUs. Grok 4.3 joined Amazon Bedrock in the June 22 weekly roundup. AWS is growing 28% with Bedrock customer spending up 170% sequentially. Amazon's $50B Stargate investment and $100B+ Anthropic compute commitment reinforce AWS as a core AI infrastructure pillar.

items.headline: Bedrock AgentCore SOC 1/2/3 compliant with new optimization capabilities — failure, intent, and trajectory insights live
items.detail: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore received an optimization capability update June 17, adding failure insights, intent insights, and trajectory insights that turn production agent traces into continuous improvement feedback loops. Web Search on AgentCore reached general availability. The service is now SOC 1, 2, and 3 compliant, enabling regulated enterprise deployments. AWS Summit New York (June 17–20) also launched AWS Continuum, AWS Context, and EC2 G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs delivering 4.6x AI inference performance.

items.headline: Cerebras-AWS partnership (June 23) adds third silicon tier for inference disaggregation alongside Trainium and NVIDIA
items.detail: The Cerebras-AWS partnership pairs Cerebras CS-3 chips (ultra-fast decode) with AWS Trainium 3 (prefill) as an inference disaggregation architecture — a third silicon tier for high-speed token generation on Amazon Bedrock. Cerebras' Q1 2026 revenue of $193.4 million (3x analyst estimates, +92% YoY) validates the AI inference demand powering AWS's multi-silicon strategy. Grok 4.3 with 1M token context also joined Bedrock (AWS Weekly Roundup June 22) alongside Claude, GPT-5.5, and other frontier models.

items.headline: Amazon $50B Stargate + $100B+ Anthropic compute commitment; AWS 28% growth; Bedrock spending +170% sequentially
items.detail: Amazon's $50 billion investment in the March 2026 Stargate funding round positions AWS as a core Stargate infrastructure partner alongside Oracle and NVIDIA. AWS Project Rainier — nearly half a million Trainium2 chips — is running Anthropic Claude workloads at scale, with Anthropic expecting more than 1 million Trainium2 chips by year end. Anthropic's $100B+/10-year expanded agreement (April 2026) secures up to 5GW of Trainium capacity. Bedrock has 125,000+ customers with 170% sequential spend growth; ~80% of Fortune 100 are AWS AI customers.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: SPCX at ~$153 (-32% from June 16 ATH of $225.64). Both Colossus 1 and Colossus 2 are committed to third-party AI companies: Anthropic ($1.25B/month), Google (~$920M/month), and Reflection AI ($150M/month starting July 1 — 5 days away). SpaceX's $60B all-stock acquisition of Cursor (Anysphere) is proceeding toward a Q3 2026 regulatory close; SpaceX and Cursor are jointly training a new model for Grok Build. Grok 4.3 is now available on Amazon Bedrock.

items.headline: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 — 5 days away; both Colossus data centers fully rented
items.detail: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI) begins July 1 — 5 days away. Reflection pays $150 million per month for GB300 GPU access via the Colossus 2 Memphis data center through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Combined with Colossus 1 (rented due to its mixed GPU composition), both Colossus data centers are fully committed to third parties, raising strategic questions about dedicated compute access for Grok AI's own workloads.

items.headline: SPCX at ~$153 (-32% from ATH); $60B Cursor acquisition proceeding toward Q3 close; SpaceX/Cursor jointly training Grok Build model
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) trades at approximately $153 — about 32% below its $225.64 all-time high reached June 16 following the Cursor announcement. The $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI) — confirmed via SEC Form 8-K — is progressing toward a Q3 2026 regulatory close. Cursor had over $2 billion ARR with more than half the Fortune 500 as clients. SpaceX and Cursor are jointly training a new model specifically for Grok Build, positioning the combined entity as a vertically integrated AI coding platform competing with Anthropic Claude Code and GitHub Copilot.

items.headline: Grok 4.3 on Amazon Bedrock with 1M token context; Colossus rental income: Anthropic $1.25B/mo, Google ~$920M/mo
items.detail: Grok 4.3 — featuring a 1 million token context window — is available on Amazon Bedrock with tool calling, structured output, and response streaming. Confirmed Colossus customer economics: Anthropic at $1.25 billion per month (through May 2029), Google at approximately $920 million per month, and Reflection AI at $150 million per month beginning July 1 through 2029. The multi-tenant model confirms SpaceX operates Colossus as a commercial AI data center platform — generating substantial infrastructure revenue independent of its model business.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: AVGO at ~$379. The $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend is payable June 30 — 4 days away — marking 14 consecutive years of dividend increases. Broadcom is fabricating OpenAI's first custom 'Titan' AI chip on TSMC's 3nm process for mass production in H2 2026 as part of Stargate. The Apollo and Blackstone-led $35B AI XPV Platform is operational, enabling 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. Q2 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8B (+143% YoY); Q3 guidance is approximately $16B.

items.headline: $0.65/share dividend payable June 30 (4 days); Q2 AI revenue $10.8B +143% YoY; Q3 guidance ~$16B; FY2027 target exceeds $100B
items.detail: Broadcom's $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend — marking 14 consecutive years of consecutive increases and 31% average annual dividend growth over 10 years — is payable June 30 to holders of record June 22. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8 billion (+143% YoY) on record quarterly revenue and free cash flow. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance is approximately $16 billion. FY2027 target exceeds $100 billion across six core custom-silicon customers. Tomahawk 6 (Davisson) — the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch — was unveiled mid-June.

items.headline: Broadcom fabricating OpenAI Titan chip on TSMC 3nm for H2 2026 Stargate deployment
items.detail: Broadcom is fabricating OpenAI's first custom 'Titan' AI accelerator on TSMC's 3nm process, with mass production targeted for H2 2026 for Stargate data center deployment. The Titan chip is designed to complement NVIDIA GPUs for specific large-scale inference workloads in Stargate. Broadcom's position as the leading custom ASIC partner for hyperscale AI silicon programs is reinforced by its six confirmed custom-silicon customers including Anthropic, Google, Meta, and OpenAI.

items.headline: Apollo and Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational — enables Anthropic 1GW+ XPU compute expansion at Fluidstack sites
items.detail: The Apollo Global Management and Blackstone-led $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform is operational since June 9 — described as the largest private financing ever executed — and enables more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The first $35B tranche facilitates Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites beginning mid-2026. Apollo president Jim Zelter appeared on CNBC June 22 to detail the deal.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: PLTR trades near its 52-week low at approximately $107 — down roughly 40% YTD despite posting blowout Q1 2026 results ($1.63B revenue, +85% YoY) and landing the US Army $10B/10-year, DHS $1B/5-year, and Treasury Department (June 23) contracts. UK parliamentary committees are urging the government to exercise its February 2027 break clause on the NHS Federated Data Platform contract. The Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline remains disrupted by the Fable/Mythos ban; the Commerce Dept deadline passed today with no public resolution.

items.headline: PLTR near 52-week low at ~$107 — down ~40% YTD; strong fundamentals (Q1 $1.63B rev, +85% YoY) not translating to stock recovery
items.detail: Palantir closed near $107 on June 26 — close to its 52-week low of $106.38 — down approximately 40% year-to-date despite exceptional operating results. Q1 2026: revenue $1.63 billion (+85% YoY), adj. EPS $0.33 (beat $0.27 consensus), US commercial revenue +133% YoY — the fastest growth in company history. FY2026 guidance raised to $7.65–7.66 billion (~71% YoY). High-profile short sellers, valuation concerns after 2025's run, and intensifying competition from frontier AI labs are the primary headwinds.

items.headline: Treasury Department contract won June 23; US Army $10B/10-year and DHS $1B/5-year deals extend federal dominance
items.detail: The US Treasury Department awarded Palantir a contract June 23 for a common API layer supporting developer platforms, workflow automation, and data analytics for IT modernization. Major 2026 federal contract wins: $10 billion 10-year US Army enterprise AI services; $1 billion 5-year DHS AI platform; $300 million USDA deal (April). Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation with usage quadrupled over 12 months. AIP is now available on Google Cloud Marketplace with BigQuery and Gemini integrations.

items.headline: Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline: Commerce Dept deadline (June 26) passed with no public response; ban Day 14
items.detail: The Fable 5/Mythos 5 export suspension continues to disrupt the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense deployment. The June 26 Commerce Department deadline imposed by bipartisan House members passed with no public response from Secretary Lutnick. The Pentagon reportedly migrated at least two-thirds of its AI workflows off Anthropic's models. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation, and Claude remains integrated for eligible workflows. Trump's statement that negotiations are 'going fine' is the most positive government signal yet. Next milestone: July 8 (Persona biometric ID pathway for potential US-only restoration).

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 with a fully rebuilt Siri powered by a custom Google Gemini model is in developer beta since WWDC June 8; public beta expected mid-July with general release September 2026. OpenAI has retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice over insufficient ChatGPT placement in the new Siri — no lawsuit filed. Apple-Perplexity acquisition talks are confirmed dead (June 23). Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral AI as an additional option. The Gemini deal remains Apple's strategic AI centerpiece.

items.headline: iOS 27 with Gemini-powered Siri in developer beta since June 8 — public beta mid-July; GA September 2026
items.detail: iOS 27 (announced at WWDC June 8) features a fully rebuilt Siri powered by a custom Google Gemini model via a three-tier routing system: on-device for fast queries, Private Cloud Compute for medium complexity, and Gemini for complex multi-step tasks. Apple pays approximately $1 billion per year for a custom Gemini model. Siri gains a dedicated app, Dynamic Island integration, full on-screen awareness, and multi-step task execution. An Extensions framework for user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini is in the developer beta but disabled pending EU Digital Markets Act regulatory clarity.

items.headline: OpenAI retained outside counsel over Siri placement; no lawsuit filed; Perplexity acquisition dead; Apple evaluating Mistral AI
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over the perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement in the new Siri. OpenAI alleges Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments and that subscription revenue 'hasn't come close' to expectations. No lawsuit has been filed as of June 26. Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are confirmed dead — management was never formally approached and no offer was made. Sherwood News reports Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional AI strategy option.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD recovered to the $522–532 range, continuing to bounce from the 6.3% decline on June 23 caused by SK Hynix slowing HBM4 expansion. Analysts from Goldman Sachs, Baird, and Barclays confirmed the selloff was a supply-chain margin concern — AMD's H2 2026 MI450 production timeline is on track. MI450 samples are confirmed shipping to OpenAI (6GW deal) and Meta ($60B/6GW partnership). AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23 at Moscone Center; Q2 financial results August 4.

items.headline: AMD recovering to $522–532 range — analysts confirm June 23 SK Hynix selloff was margin concern, not demand signal; MI450 on track
items.detail: AMD shares continued recovering toward the $522–532 range, rebounding from the 6.3% decline on June 23 triggered by SK Hynix slowing HBM4 memory expansion to redirect capacity toward conventional DRAM. Analysts at Goldman Sachs (Buy, $450 target), Baird ($625), and Barclays (Street-high $665) characterized the selloff as a supply-chain gross margin concern for AMD's HBM-intensive MI450 GPU roadmap rather than a demand or deployment delay signal. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to OpenAI and Meta on track.

items.headline: MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta; AMD Advancing AI 2026 July 22–23; Q2 results August 4
items.detail: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 GPU samples are shipping to lead customers OpenAI (6GW partnership, beginning with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026) and Meta ($60B/5-year, 6GW, including a co-designed custom MI450). AMD denied reports of MI455X delays; Helios rack systems remain on target for H2 2026 production. AMD Advancing AI 2026 is a two-day event July 22–23 at Moscone Center in San Francisco — expected to cover the formal MI450 launch, EPYC Venice Zen 6 CPUs, Helios rack confirmation, and MI500 series preview for 2027. Q2 2026 financial results: August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Comet AI browser is globally available on Mac, iOS, Android, and Windows. Computer tools — inline editing, task controls, and live credit tracking — rolled out to Pro subscribers. Max subscribers can choose their model (Opus 4.6 default or Sonnet 4.5). CEO Aravind Srinivas confirmed a 2028 IPO target, explicitly independent of Anthropic and OpenAI timelines. Apple-Perplexity acquisition talks are confirmed dead. ARR reached $500M (April 2026, +335% YoY); 100M+ MAU; $20B valuation; $750M Azure infrastructure deal.

items.headline: Comet browser globally available on all platforms; Computer tools for Pro subscribers; model choice for Max subscribers
items.detail: Perplexity's Comet AI browser is globally available on Mac, iOS, Android, and Windows. Computer agentic tools — inline editing, task controls, and live credit tracking — have rolled out to Pro subscribers. Max subscribers can now choose their preferred AI model inside Comet: Opus 4.6 as default or Sonnet 4.5 as an alternative. Deep Research now creates presentations and structured outputs. Comet reached #3 in the App Store within 48 hours of its iOS launch. Comet Enterprise launched March 17 across two commercial tiers.

items.headline: Apple-Perplexity acquisition confirmed dead; CEO reaffirms 2028 IPO; Perplexity Computer in Microsoft 365; $500M ARR
items.detail: Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are confirmed dead as of June 23 — Perplexity management was never formally approached and no formal offer was made. CEO Aravind Srinivas publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO (June 9 CNBC interview). Perplexity Computer is live as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams — competing with Microsoft Copilot inside Microsoft 365 itself. ARR reached $500M (April 2026, +335% YoY) with 100M+ MAU and a $750M Azure infrastructure deal.

companies.name: CoreWeave
